title: daucus pumilus subsp. minusculus
intro: Daucus pumilus subsp. minusculus, commonly known as wild carrot or pygmy wild carrot, is a small, annual plant species belonging to the carrot family Apiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antispasmodic, diuretic, and expectorant effects, making it useful in treating digestive issues, bronchitis, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Daucus pumilus subsp. minusculus include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving respiratory function, among others. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
